vehicles.slot: Slot at base of gear selector on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot at base of gear selector on center console.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot at base of gear selector on center console.
vehicles.notes: Manual: depress clutch, select N — no shift lock.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk (right side under panel) or under rear seat
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal near firewall (+), black ground bolt on strut tower (−)
battery.warnings: Always use hood terminals — never jump directly at battery. AGM rated jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Near base of gear selector on console — trim piece may need removal
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock slot — Near base of gear selector on console — trim piece may need removal.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move the ZF 8HP selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk right side under panel. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
BMW/ZF 8HP: battery is typically NOT under the hood — in trunk right side under panel. Always use the hood jump terminals.
vehicles.notes: ZF 8HP is reliable. Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. xDrive AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk right side under panel
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals only. AGM jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Trim cap on console near selector — pry to expose slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Trim cap on console near selector — pry to expose slot.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood, driver side (no trunk battery on E46).
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.

vehicles.notes: Use hood jump terminals — no need to access trunk battery directly.

battery.location: Under hood, driver side (no trunk battery on E46)
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals, easy access
battery.warnings: E46 is one of the few BMWs with a conventional under-hood battery.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim near base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot under trim near base.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk under panel. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. xDrive AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.warnings: Use hood terminals only. AGM jump pack.

vehicles.notes: M5 E60 uses SMG — REQUIRES POWER to shift to neutral. Jump battery FIRST on M5.
vehicles.warn: M5 E60 SMG: jump battery first. No mechanical override on SMG.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. xDrive AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

knownIssues.title: M5 E60 SMG Has No Mechanical Neutral Override
knownIssues.detail: The M5 E60 uses a Single Mono-clutch Gearbox (SMG) that is fully electronic. There is NO mechanical override to shift to neutral. The 12V battery MUST be jumped first before any shift to neutral is possible. Flatbed is the only safe option with a completely dead battery.

battery.location: In trunk (right side under panel)
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals. M5 E60 SMG: must restore power before any neutral shift — no mechanical override.

vehicles.slot: Base of selector COLUMN — small slot under a cap (iDrive electronic selector)
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
This vehicle uses a COLUMN-MOUNTED shifter — the shift lock slot is on the STEERING COLUMN, not the floor console.
Locate the slot on the steering column — Base of selector COLUMN — small slot under a cap (iDrive electronic selector).
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing the brake pedal.
Move the column shifter to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is under the rear seat — but use the jump terminals UNDER THE HOOD to connect your jump pack. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and move column shifter to N normally.
vehicles.notes: iDrive selector — slot is at column base, not console. Air suspension — check clearance.
vehicles.warn: Air suspension: verify clearance before flatbed.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. xDrive AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.

knownIssues.title: Air Suspension Clearance Risk
knownIssues.detail: Air suspension can drop to bump stops when power is lost, making flatbed loading difficult or damaging the undercarriage. If possible, restore 12V power to raise suspension before loading. Check clearance carefully.

knownIssues.title: iDrive Electronic Shifter
knownIssues.detail: This generation uses an electronic column-mounted iDrive selector. The shift lock slot is at the base of the selector column, not on the center console. Operators unfamiliar with this layout often cannot locate it.

battery.location: Under rear seat or in trunk — varies
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals only. Air suspension drops with dead battery. AGM jump pack.

shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot near gear selector base on center console.

Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.

Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.

vehicles.notes: All E83 X3 are xDrive AWD.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — xDrive AWD is full-time, cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals

Locate the shift lock slot — Trim piece at electronic selector base.
Insert a thin key or small flathead into the slot.
Press firmly and hold.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move the ZF 8HP selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — Under hood. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
BMW/ZF 8HP: battery is typically NOT under the hood — under hood. Always use the hood jump terminals.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — xDrive AWD is full-time, cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: HYBRID/PHEV: Use 12V auxiliary battery ONLY for jumping. NEVER access the high-voltage battery pack.
FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
AIR SUSPENSION: May lower/raise unexpectedly. Verify ride height is stable before loading on flatbed. Some models have a 'jack mode' or 'transport mode' — check with owner if possible.

battery.location: Under hood
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals
battery.warnings: AGM jump pack. PHEV: 12V only.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim at selector base
shift: Turn the BMW iX / i4 / i5 / i7 to ON/Ready mode.
Shift lock slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim at selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
Press the brake pedal while holding the slot.
Move selector to N.
FLATBED ONLY — do not tow with driven wheels on the ground.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Open the trunk — battery is ix: under hood. i4/i5/i7: in trunk. Connect jump pack. 12V AUXILIARY BATTERY ONLY — never touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to N normally.
FLATBED ONLY.
vehicles.notes: NEVER touch the high-voltage system.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Never tow with driven wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain
towNotes: ELECTRIC VEHICLE: FLATBED ONLY — never tow with any driven wheels on the ground. Regenerative braking system can damage motor/inverter.
If vehicle must be moved short distance without flatbed, use dollies under ALL driven wheels.
12V battery ONLY for jumping — NEVER touch or access the high-voltage (HV) system. HV components are marked with ORANGE cables/covers.

knownIssues.title: FLATBED ONLY — High Voltage System
knownIssues.detail: 800V high-voltage system. Never attempt to tow with wheels on ground. Never connect jumper cables to HV components. 12V auxiliary battery only for powering electronics.

knownIssues.title: Battery Location Varies by Model
knownIssues.detail: iX: 12V under hood. i4/i5/i7: 12V in trunk. Always verify before attempting to jump.

battery.location: iX: under hood. i4/i5/i7: in trunk
battery.jumpTerminals: See location. 12V auxiliary terminals only.
battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV orange cables. 12V auxiliary only for all jump operations.

vehicles.slot: Slot near gear selector base
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot near gear selector base.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk, right side under panel.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot near gear selector base.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk, right side under panel
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals.

vehicles.slot: Slot at selector base — trim piece may need removal
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: Slot at selector base — trim piece may need removal.
Pry off the cap or cover if present using your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key (or a thin flathead) into the slot and press firmly downward.
While holding the slot, press the brake pedal.
Move the selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove key from slot and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk, right side under panel. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If power cannot be restored, use the shift lock slot above — Slot at selector base — trim piece may need removal.
vehicles.notes: Manual: clutch to N.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk, right side under panel
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals.

vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — slot under trim near base
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the electronic selector override: Electronic selector — slot under trim near base.
Remove trim panel or cover if present — use a trim pry tool or your fingernail at the edge.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the exposed slot.
Press firmly inward and hold.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Press the N button or move the electronic selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — In trunk, under panel. Use an AGM-compatible jump pack — this vehicle has an AGM battery.
Once 12V power is restored, press brake and select N normally on the electronic selector.
Electronic selectors require power — mechanical override is a last resort only.
vehicles.notes: Shared platform with Toyota Supra.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: RWD: Lift from REAR. Rear (drive) wheels are lifted in the air. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLE: Use flatbed. Check ground clearance before attempting any wheel-lift — front splitter/rear diffuser damage is common.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

battery.location: In trunk, under panel
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood: red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Use hood terminals.

vehicles.slot: Slot at base of gear selector, under small cover or accessed by lifting shift boot surround
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: lift the shift boot surround or locate the small slot cover at the selector base.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press the cam or lever to release.
While holding, press brake. Move to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch fully and move gear lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is under the hood on E36 — standard location. Connect jump pack.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air.
vehicles.notes: E36 has battery under the hood — unlike later BMWs. Manual trans: depress clutch, Neutral.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air. AWD not available on E36.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
Battery under hood on E36.

battery.location: Under hood
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — standard terminals
battery.warnings: E36 has battery under hood, unlike later BMWs.

vehicles.slot: Slot at selector base, accessed under shift boot or small trim cover
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
AUTOMATIC: locate the slot at the selector base under the shift boot trim.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot and press firmly.
While holding, press brake. Move to Neutral.
MANUAL: depress clutch and move lever to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery is in the TRUNK right side under a panel — NOT under the hood.
Use hood jump terminals: red fused terminal near firewall (+), black ground bolt (−).
Use AGM-compatible jump pack. Never jump directly at the trunk battery.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air.
vehicles.notes: Battery in trunk right side. Always use hood jump terminals.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: Battery in trunk — use hood jump terminals.
RWD: lift from rear.

battery.location: Trunk, right side under panel
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: Always use hood terminals — never jump directly at trunk battery. AGM jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Slot at column-mounted selector or console base depending on trim
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
E38 uses a column-mounted gear selector — shift lock slot is at the STEERING COLUMN BASE.
Insert a thin key or flathead into the slot on the column.
Press firmly while pressing brake. Move column selector to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery under the hood or in trunk — varies by trim. Use hood jump terminals.
Use AGM-compatible jump pack.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air.
Air suspension on some trims — may drop with dead battery. Jump first.
vehicles.notes: Column shifter — slot is on steering column, not console. Air suspension on some trims.
vehicles.warn: Air suspension: may drop to bump stops with dead battery.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear — rear (drive) wheels in the air.
towNotes: Column shifter — slot on steering column.
Air suspension: jump battery first.

battery.location: Under hood or trunk — varies
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — red fused terminal (+), black ground bolt (−)
battery.warnings: AGM jump pack. Air suspension may drop with dead battery.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
Once 12V power is restored, shift to Neutral normally.
xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred.

vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred — permanent AWD.

battery.location: Under hood or trunk (varies by generation — E84: trunk; F48/U11: under hood or trunk)
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood — remote jump terminals; or trunk battery directly
battery.warnings: AGM battery — use AGM-compatible jump pack. Verify battery location.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
DCT: REQUIRES 12V power — restore battery first.
Locate the shift lock slot near the selector base.
Insert key or thin flathead, press firmly, hold, press brake, move to Neutral.
DCT without power will not release — always jump first.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood.
DCT: must restore 12V power before shifting.
Once powered, shift to Neutral normally.
xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.notes: DCT: requires power. xDrive: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: DCT: restore power before shifting. xDrive: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from front. xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred.
towNotes: FWD: lift from front.
xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred.
DCT: cannot shift without 12V power.

battery.location: Under hood or trunk — verify before jumping
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood remote terminals or trunk
battery.warnings: AGM. DCT: must restore power before shifting.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — under the hood or trunk (verify location).
Once 12V power is restored, shift to Neutral normally.
vehicles.notes: Always xDrive AWD — flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED — permanent xDrive AWD. Wheel-lift with dollies if no flatbed.
towNotes: Permanent xDrive AWD — flatbed is safest.
Wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle if no flatbed.

battery.location: Trunk (most X4) — verify before jumping
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood remote positive terminal + chassis ground; or trunk battery directly
battery.warnings: Battery typically in trunk on X4. Use under-hood remote jump posts if available. AGM — use AGM-compatible jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — TRUNK on most X6 generations.
X6 battery is typically in the trunk — check under the rear cargo floor.
Under-hood remote positive jump post is available on the driver side of the engine bay.

vehicles.warn: Battery in TRUNK — check under cargo floor. Remote jump post under hood. xDrive AWD — flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED PREFERRED — permanent xDrive AWD.
towNotes: Permanent xDrive AWD — flatbed is safest.
Battery in trunk — use under-hood remote jump post if available.
Wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle if no flatbed.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Trunk — Remote Jump Post Under Hood
knownIssues.detail: The X6 battery is in the trunk under the cargo floor. BMW provides a remote positive (+) jump post in the engine bay on the driver side. Use the remote post and a good chassis ground rather than opening the trunk. The trunk battery can also be accessed directly.

battery.location: TRUNK — under cargo floor. Remote jump post under hood (driver side engine bay).
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood remote post (+) + chassis ground, OR trunk battery directly
battery.warnings: Battery in trunk. AGM — use AGM-compatible jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack to battery — TRUNK (driver side, under cargo area).
Z3 battery is in the trunk — do NOT look under the hood.
Once powered, shift to Neutral normally.
Manual: depress clutch, select Neutral.
vehicles.notes: Battery in TRUNK. Manual: depress clutch. High-value collectible — flatbed preferred.
vehicles.warn: Battery in TRUNK. High-value vehicle — flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. Flatbed preferred.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
Battery in TRUNK.
High-value — flatbed preferred.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Trunk
knownIssues.detail: The Z3 12V battery is in the trunk on the driver side under the cargo area. There is no battery under the hood.

battery.location: TRUNK — driver side under cargo area
battery.jumpTerminals: Trunk — 12V battery terminals
battery.warnings: Battery in trunk.

vehicles.trans: Single-speed electric (or range extender 2-cylinder scooter engine on REx)
vehicles.slot: Electronic selector — joystick-style on center console
shift: Turn vehicle to ON/Ready mode.
The i3 uses a joystick-style electronic selector on the center console.
Push the joystick TOWARD YOU (rearward) to select Neutral.
Hold for 2-3 seconds. Confirm N on the instrument cluster.
If 12V power is lost: restore 12V auxiliary power first (battery under hood in frunk area).
Once powered, use the joystick to select N.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain.
Connect jump pack to 12V auxiliary battery — under the front hood (small frunk area).
12V AUXILIARY ONLY — NEVER touch the high-voltage battery pack.
Once 12V is restored, push joystick rearward to select Neutral.
Enable Tow mode via iDrive if available: Vehicle Settings > Service > Tow.
vehicles.notes: Carbon fiber body (CFRP) — very light. 12V auxiliary in frunk. HV battery under floor. Flatbed only. REx models have small 2-cylinder range extender but it does NOT drive the wheels.
vehicles.warn: FLATBED ONLY. Carbon fiber body — use padded equipment only. 12V in frunk.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — electric drivetrain. Carbon fiber body requires padded ramps.
towNotes: ELECTRIC: FLATBED ONLY.
Carbon fiber (CFRP) body — never use metal hooks or chains against the body. Padded equipment only.
12V auxiliary in frunk — NEVER touch HV battery (orange cables under floor).
REx models still FLATBED ONLY — range extender does not drive wheels.

knownIssues.title: Carbon Fiber Body — Special Equipment Required
knownIssues.detail: The i3 is constructed from carbon fiber reinforced plastic (CFRP). Never use metal chains, hooks, or straps directly against the body — it will crack. Use padded or soft-loop equipment only. Load onto flatbed with padded ramps.

knownIssues.title: 12V Battery in Frunk
knownIssues.detail: The i3 12V auxiliary battery is in the front hood area (frunk). Open the front hood — it is a small storage area, not an engine bay. The HV battery is under the floor and must NEVER be touched.

battery.location: FRUNK (front hood area) — small 12V auxiliary battery
battery.jumpTerminals: Frunk — 12V auxiliary terminals
battery.warnings: NEVER touch HV battery. Carbon body — padded equipment only. Flatbed only.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Battery location varies: E63/E64 battery is in the TRUNK. F12/F13 battery in trunk.
Open trunk, access battery directly OR use under-hood remote jump post if present.
Once powered, shift to Neutral.
xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.notes: Battery typically in TRUNK on most 6 Series. Remote jump post may be under hood. High value.
vehicles.warn: Battery in TRUNK. xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: RWD: lift from rear. xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred. Flatbed preferred for all due to vehicle value.
towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
Battery in trunk — use under-hood remote post if available.
High-value vehicle — flatbed preferred.

knownIssues.title: Battery in Trunk
knownIssues.detail: Most 6 Series have the 12V battery in the trunk. A remote positive jump post may be in the engine bay. Always check the trunk for the battery.

battery.location: TRUNK — under cargo floor or cargo area
battery.jumpTerminals: Trunk battery directly OR under-hood remote post (+) + chassis ground
battery.warnings: Battery in trunk. AGM — use AGM-compatible jump pack.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base on center console
shift: Turn ignition to ON.
Locate the shift lock release: small slot near gear selector base on center console.
Pry off cap if present.
Insert key or thin flathead and press firmly downward.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move selector from Park to Neutral.
Remove tool and replace cap.
neutral: Apply parking brake.
Connect jump pack — battery is in the TRUNK.
Use under-hood remote positive jump post (driver side engine bay) + chassis ground.
Or open trunk and connect directly to battery.
xDrive AWD: flatbed preferred.

vehicles.warn: Battery in TRUNK. Very high-value — flatbed preferred.

towNotes: RWD: lift from rear.
Very high-value — flatbed strongly preferred.

battery.location: TRUNK. Remote jump post under hood (driver side engine bay).
battery.jumpTerminals: Under hood remote post (+) + chassis ground, OR trunk battery directly
